reviews.review_text:
"God of War | 2018 | PC | Played for: 55h

I was not invested in the series (1st entry for me), nor the slasher type game and neither in the Norse mythology. By the end of the game I set my eyes on the sequel Ragnarok, and was compelled to 100% the game.

Graphics are stunning and very well optimised, artwork and sound-work are superb. Gameplay at beginning seems dull, by the end is ok. Except Valkyries bosses, on normal difficulty nothing seems to challenge, while on hard enemies are damage sponges. Petty that the light and heavy runic attacks can not be set to "random". 

Overall it is a well regarded and recommended game by most for a reason, I went sceptical into it and was hooked until the end.

Story: 8,5/10
Gameplay: 7,5/10
Graphics/Physics: 9/10
Music/Sound: 9/10
Final Score: 8,33/10

A simple task begins a much more involved and deep narrative that takes heavy inspiration from The Last of Us Part I in terms of gameplay, narrative themes, and structure. The combat is thrilling, feeling visceral and engaging while still retaining some of the spirit of the original series. Beautiful landscapes and detailed character models really bring the world to life, making this one of the most beautiful games still worth playing to this day (art design over graphical fidelity). The characters are awesome, featuring a more complex version of Kratos and amazing performances from the voice actors, even though I do miss Kratos’ original voice actor Terrence C. Carson.
Played it for a bit, I'd say it's pretty good. Very accurate to average day to day living in Scandinavia.

I loooove Norse mythology and this was so fun to play! The gameplay was fun and 100% the game didn’t feel like a slog. I loved the Valkyrie fights a lot 😍 Overall enjoyable game and I cant wait to play the next installment!
This game has an incredible story and great combat. I would request this game to people who love puzzles and third-person melee combat.
The gameplay was fun and the story was interesting,

This is a great game and I highly recommend it, Im on my secound playthrough currently. It has great replay value, its challenging, long and really makes u feel like a dad, caring for atreus, My only nitpicks are the lack of enemies and ALFHEIM, the only trash part of this game, the dark elve king made me smash my keyboard, both times

God of War is an amazing game. It is visually stunning, and the relationship between Kratos and his son, Atreus, is written so well. You embark on an adventure to fulfill the final wish of Kratos' late wife, Faye. Throughout the journey, you watch their bond grow and improve as they slowly uncover Kratos' dark past.

I know I am clearly, VASTLY outnumbered in this opinion, but I genuinely did not enjoy the game

The gameplay is fun, and obviously a ton of work went into the game as a whole. From locations to pacing to progression to graphics to feel. This is definitely the kind of game big studious should be making instead of soulless, committee designed slop

But this game gets unreal praise for it's story and characters and I just did not enjoy either. The characters are fine, if a little one note, but the story just absolutely falls apart in the back half when personalities start swiveling on a dime and story beats started getting pulled out of nowhere moments before they were relevant

I know there are like 150,000 people on steam alone who would disagree with me, but if you're thinking it might not be for you, and hoping to find confirmation in the negative reviews, here it is

It wasn't for me. It might not be for you
The game does a great job with pacing until you get to the end and then its just grind simulator.
I wish it was norse myths only. They didnt mix the two well. Still good game.

It might be a little late for me to play this since I never had a proper device before, but this game is an absolute beauty. Even though the story felt a bit too short, it was incredibly enjoyable and easy to understand. Not to mention, this was my first time playing any game from the God of War franchise. 

note : fk u sigrun
This is one of the best games that i have played. The story and the graphics are great.

Checks all the boxes.

Story-Stoic daddy simulator with humor, BOY gets a little emo at times but hey he's just a boy. 
Graphics-Rivals any of the top, even being 8 years old. 
Combat-Never gets old, beating the crap out of enemies and bosses, dodging/parry feels rewarding.
Optimization-Runs super smooth at 5120x2160 Maxed out, No DLSS needed.

Sadly Sony has said we will not be getting any more Sony exclusive titles for PC. =(  

One way or another I will be playing the upcoming Sony exclusives on PC eventually. =)
Just completed full story after 21 hours of gameplay. What. A. Game.

I don't know why did this game become goty. Technically it is pretty good with impressive visuals but this is all there is to it. The combat is mid since the enemies have boring patterns, bosses use the same 2-3 moves and the enemie variety is incredibly low. The level design is also mid and you spend most of the time looking around to solve puzzles which just add more meaningless gameplay time to the game. The story is also very cliche with the typical strict father/naive son relationship and you won't see any exciting plot twist or anything interesting other than some impressive cutscenes. Rdr2 really got robbed from goty if you ask me.

One of the best story games with combat just as good. Must play.

Also father simulator.

it's God of War come on now I love everything the gameplay the story especially the story and of course I love the Dad of War himself Kratos and his BOY I absolute recommend this game to anyone who sees this you have to play it as it truly is a phenomenal experience but make sure to take your time with it and not rush through the game you can play however you like but trust me taking it slow is the best way you can experience this wonderful game

One of the best games I played.
Great Story, great graphics, great comeback of a beloved character.
Highly Recommended.

I'm not that far into the story. After I unlocked the hub area I got distracted with side quests. From what I've seen so far though its pretty good. 

The main point I wanted to emphasize with this review is the game's performance. I'm rendering it in 4K at native resolution (without DLSS) on max settings and its the game preforms beautifully. There's a small to moderate load on my CPU, my VRAM is always maxed out, and my GPU never goes below 80%, but I have yet to noticeably drop below 60fps while playing.  The game runs at an extremely consistent framerate, looks amazing, and honestly this kind of performance is very rare in modern games. It uses every resource available to the maximum potential which delivers amazing results. It's all the more impressive that this is a console port as well. Whoever was in charge of optimization really knew what they were doing.
im doing a new game + playthrough and for a damn good reason, this game is absolutely awesome!

I ♥♥♥♥♥♥' love this game. It's beautiful. It's FAT (i.e., lots of content for the price of the game). It's funny, and moving, and a great introduction to the GoW franchise if you're (in my case) younger than the originals/didn't have a childhood.
The gameplay and story are excellent and I think it's well optimised. It is a bit short though
Amazing game. But really think carefully before picking "Give Me God Of War" difficulty. Completed it, but damn it was frustrating sometimes.
Loved the game on PS4 when it came out then on here it was just as good but ran so much better. If its on sale I highly recommend
absolutely LOVE this game so far, but.. when i interact with something and kratos just stands there. I can't help but scream and shout..

[b] I should probably open with that I have never played a God of War game before this one so there's that. [/b]

I do recommend the game but mostly on the basis of the story. It's a pretty touching story of a son learning about his father slowly, as well as the father learning about how to approach his son. It's basically a road trip.

[h1] Gameplay: 4/10 [/h1]
Probably just not for me. I've played through the Batman: Arkham series. As well as Spiderman, Elden Ring, Star wars Jedi Survivor games so I have experience with similar combat systems and they do it MUCH better. In all of those games I was looking for excuses to fight things, doing all the combat challenges I could and having fun with the skill tree and all the unlocks therein. God of War (2018) was pretty lackluster. It felt like i was buttonmashing my way through every single combat encounter. The enemy variety is incredibly limited in 90% of combat encounters, every fight was just throw all of your abilities at as many guys as you can as often as you can and reap the rewards. Occasionally you can fight a mini boss but most of them are copy and paste and that is not remotely an exaggeration. Also the puzzles were not very fun or engaging. Puzzles are pretty hit or miss for me in most games and this game was definitely a miss. most puzzles are throw your axe (or fire an arrow) at this thing OR move a block so you can stand on it. Again, pretty lackluster overall.

[h1] Story: 9/10 [/h1]
I probably would have dropped the game if I did not find the story as interesting as I did, but I am also a sucker for mythology. I teared up at some of the scenes with great payoff and it really felt like the characters were people. A father grieving the loss of his wife, a son grieving the loss of his mother. Both of them having to process that grief all while learning to coexist with each other (the game explains early on that Kratos was usually busy hunting and whatnot so they are actually not that familiar with each other). I am definitely picking up the sequel because the game has a very Part 1 of ? vibe to it and I want to see where it goes.

[h2] In Conclusion 7/10 [/h2]
If you are looking for a fun game with a good story, its pretty good. It only takes about 20 hours to beat so it is not a huge time sink if you don't want it to be, but it has enough side content you can definitely get more out of it if you REALLY want to. I can't forgive what i felt was a lackluster combat system (compared to other AAA titles) or the uninteresting puzzles [b] BUT [/b] i can give a lot of credit for the amazing story, characters, and art in the game. The art is something I did not mention earlier because I don't believe its worth talking about outside of acknowledging in closing, The models look very good and don't seem far fetched or downplayed, However they did not WOW me enough to actually count for extra scoring personally.

The combat is fun and it keeps throwing new stuff at you until the end of the game. The levels are very linear and the movement is very limited too but it's still fun to explore here and there. The puzzles have some variety to them, although they are very simple. The difficulty levels suck because all they do is basically giving more health to enemies and making their attacks stronger. Felt a little bit too long for what it is but it's still alright. The story is a hit or miss but the characters are pretty good and it was interesting to follow their development. Runs pretty good on my pc while looking pretty good. Overall it's alright, I liked it.

deserved GOTY 100% well made story and also amazing combat overall great to revive the franchise
10/10

Это как смотреть новые фильмы со старыми героями боевиков из детства. Сталлоне и Чак Норрис всё ещё выходят на экран и тащат, хотя песок из них уже высыпается тоннами.
И здесь - та же история.

Кратос всё ещё раздаёт врагам по первое число, крушит, рвёт, ломает, но… он уже не тот. В нём чувствуется возраст, тяжесть прожитых лет, и это даже добавляет игре шарма. Он стал медленнее, мрачнее, глубже - но когда начинается бой, ты понимаешь: старый волк всё ещё помнит, как охотиться.

Да, былые времена не вернуть. Да, юношеского безумия уже нет. Но игра всё равно держит планку так, что многим молодым тайтлам остаётся только нервно курить в углу. Атмосфера, постановка, боёвка, история - всё работает как надо.

10 из 10. Старики всё ещё в игре. И это чертовски приятно.

This was an incredible experience.
The combat is smooth and brutal, the story hits hard, and the one-shot camera makes everything feel immersive. Kratos and Atreus' journey kept me hooked from start to finish. Easily one of the best single-player games I've played.
I definitely recommend playing New Game+ as well- the unique gear and added challenges make it feel like a fresh experience all over again
A game with amazing story, a fun and rewarding combat system, and amazing music. Side note it also ran really well.

I like when big snake speaks and breaks my eardrums
only took 32 hours to fully 100 percent the game 
love the name reveal for BOY at the end 
Kratos is the GOAT
I hate the dark elf enemies 
Fun and Beautiful game 9/10
great game that everyone should experience. i will be buying Ragnarok

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ amazing game. started feb 14 and finished may 30th due to procastination and adhd but i finally locked tf in and finished the game and it was a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ piece of art truly a van gough of video games,

This is my first time playing GOW and now GOW is now 1 of my fave games of all time

Witcher 3
Elden Ring,Darksouls 3
Cyberpunk 2077
GOD OF WAR
I had seen ads for this but never really considered getting it. I finally bought it and I have been missing out for years!
The story and character development is amazing.
Beautiful game overall as expected but just finished New Game+ about to get back into Invaldis workshop hopefully but I heavily recommend GOW 2018 and GOWR

I normally don't attempt to 100% the game but something in this one compelled me to. Once you get used to the fighting mechanics, it becomes quite enjoyable, though I only grasped the rotations when faced against actually tough opponents.
Also, the dialogues. Mimir with his typical English humour has kept the atmosphere at bay and Brok... Brok is lovely, his sarcastic comments have made me lol a couple of times. Overall a fun game with a bit of Norse Mythology if you're into this stuff. I wasn't, but the game kept it lighthearted enough to not infodump, but interesting enough that I've researched some of the stuff myself to get to know the story better. There were some repeated, boring sequences or annoying enemies, but overall I'd say I enjoyed playing it, both for the story and the gameplay.

God of War has every good aspect that an epic game should have: stunning graphics, epic and engaging story, excellent soundtrack and voiceover, interesting narrative, and really good gameplay.  Lots of extras that are actually worth doing and you'll miss some great game moments if you rush past them.  I think I paid  10 bucks for this game.  Ridiculously awesome value.  I totally loved this game.
God of war is one of my all time favorite story games. The acting, gameplay, graphics and so much is so great. I wish more games had the depth that this game does.

God of War (2018) is an absolute masterpiece.

Honestly, going into this, I expected a typical, rage-fueled action game. What I actually got was one of the best father-son stories ever told in gaming, wrapped inside an incredibly satisfying action-RPG. Watching Kratos try so hard to be a better father to Atreus—while being constantly haunted by his own violent past—gives the game a massive emotional heart. And once Mimir joins the crew and starts telling Norse myths from your boat, the world just completely comes alive.

The combat is just pure fun. The Leviathan Axe might be one of the most satisfying weapons ever put in a video game—throwing it and recalling it back to your hand with that heavy thud never gets old. Mixing up heavy runic attacks, shield bashes, and the later-game weapons makes you feel like an absolute powerhouse.

If you haven't played it yet, do yourself a favor and pick it up. It’s easily a *10/10* and worth every single hour.

It was my first GoW into the series as i have no plans or means to play the previous ones, but my god, i went into this thinking im gonna miss out on so much without context yet i dont feel left out. This game deserves its glaze, the combat is simple yet flashy, the exploration is super nice, i really liked the little metroidvania touch as well. Had fun playing through it.,

This game was amazing. Im still currently working my way through the optional things at endgame  ie Valkyries - but the game had me hooked from the start. 

Narratively beautiful. Ive played every iteration of God Of War, starting from childhood and now as an adult Watching Kratos struggle to find his footing as a father hit me right in the feels. I love that each character had complexities and different dynamics with one another. Felt very immersive & satisfying. One small detail i loved (Trying to avoid spoilers for future players) is an emotional scene where we get to see Kratos genuinely anxious. I turned my camera out of curiosity and saw him lowkey shaking and he began pacing. It was so vulnerable and real, I loved the detail added there.

Music/Sound design in general gets a 10/10 for me. I don't have the right words other than encapsulating. Great work here from all the music sources, sound effects, monster sounds, etc! 

Dragons! Dwarves! Monsters! Rewarding Exploration! Feeling badass with all the builds you can rock! 

Great game, wish I played it way sooner!

A great parenting sim, would recomend for anyone expecting. 
10/10 good story and great combat mechanics.
BING BONG

I started God of War pretty late, and it was my first entry into the series, but it completely won me over.
The story was easily the highlight for me. Kratos and Atreus’ journey was emotional, engaging, and kept me invested from beginning to end. Even after finishing the game, I still can’t stop thinking about it. The ending left me incredibly excited to jump into Ragnarök.
The world is beautiful, and the artwork and environmental design are absolutely stunning. Every area feels carefully crafted and memorable. My only real complaint is that I wish the game offered a bit more freedom when it came to exploration, as I sometimes wanted to wander off and discover more on my own.
The combat was more challenging than I usually prefer, which occasionally frustrated me, but it never stopped me from enjoying the experience. In fact, overcoming those tougher encounters made the victories feel even more rewarding.
Overall, God of War exceeded my expectations. Between its incredible story, gorgeous world, memorable characters, and satisfying gameplay, it’s an easy recommendation and one of the best action adventure games I’ve played. I can’t wait to continue the journey in Ragnarök.

I played the original trilogy first, and Kratos is probably my favourite video game character of all time, but this game raised my Kratos stock through the roof. The thought of a soulless, vengeful god-killer being a trying single father who wants to set an honorable example for his son in his older age is some of the best storytelling I've ever seen played out in a video game. The gameplay is also superb, with my only real gripe being how close the third-person camera is during combat.

All around a near-perfect experience, kudos to Santa Monica for taking the series in a different direction while still doing my goat the proper justice.
Played on ps back in the day i enjoyed it then, i enjoy it now. Great story, characters, gameplay, and graphics. Decent length that doesn't overstay its welcome. Ran really well on my rig.

Very glad I came back and wrapped this game up. Played about 14 hours in a few weeks (which is consistent for me) because this game just had me hooked. Great combat, great story, great dynamic, and it runs/looks great. 

HIghly recommend and adding Ragnarok to my WishList!

I’ve always been a massive fan of the franchise, and this game completely exceeded my expectations. 
If you love Norse mythology, this is an absolute dream come true. From the moment I started playing, I was completely immersed in the stunning world, and intense combat that this game offers. The graphics are breathtaking, the narrative is emotionally charged and well-written, and the combat is beautifully crafted.
Great game overall with a great story, BUT bad optimization in certain areas. No option for dynamic resolution definitely made it harder to enjoy how it should be.

I mean....it's GoW 2018, only it looks and runs like 80% better. What else could you ask for. It's like saying "i don't know if you knew this...but Breaking Bad....is a pretty good show".  Yes. Yes it is.  This game makes up 1 half of the best story ever told in gaming imo.  So if you haven't played this and ragnarok, wth are you doing with your life. Play it.
game was very good. I liked it alot! However,, i still believe it robbed red dead redemption 2 for game of the year 2018. 9.5/10

I've taken some time to get to this game, but was worth it. Atreus is a great companion to have, i know there is a head about to join soon, can't wait for it. Overall, one of the best games, and the history of Kratos and how the world reacts to him is perfect.

I started this game a couple of times over the years but always put it down because the beginning really didn't grab me. 
Recently I have been playing through my backlog of games and this one came up. I gave it another try and the same thing happened, the beginning didn't grab me and I almost put it down. I decided to push through however and oh my gosh am I glad I did. 
This game is AMAZING for so many reasons that only someone who has played it can understand. I have never played a game with this level of imagination, storytelling, and fun mechanics. If you haven't played this game but you like gaming, you owe it to yourself to try it. It is a truly special game.
One of the best Games I have played Awesome story and character development
Very good gameplay and story, good performance, somewhat grindy at times in late game.

Having played the original Greek trilogy, I was incredibly excited to see Kratos again—but I wasn’t prepared for just how much this game would blow me away.

The shift from a rage-fueled warrior to a father trying to connect with his son, Atreus, is handled beautifully. This dynamic shows a completely different, deeply human side of Kratos that we've never seen before. Watching their bond grow throughout the journey is easily the highlight of the story.

On the gameplay front, the combat system is a major departure from the older, fixed-camera hack-and-slash titles. The over-the-shoulder, third-person perspective felt a bit jarring at first, but once I adjusted to it, the combat felt incredibly weighty, satisfying, and precise.

Outside of the main story, there is a ton of great content. The boss battles are cinematic and intense, and the secret fights (especially hunting down the Valkyries) offer a fantastic, rewarding challenge.

If you are a fan of the classic games, or just love exceptional storytelling and combat, this is a must-play. I cannot wait to jump into God of War Ragnarök next!

Its simply masterpiece. The story is quite simple but the journey is truly extraordinary. I really enjoy the combat mechanics in this game, its not difficult, quite easy for beginner in fact. The Cinematic Scenes are the best part of this game in my opinion. Absolute Cinema.

The only complaint i have is for Atreus, who was incredibly annoying throughout the entire run lol.

I'm adventuring my life in souls like (I know this game it's not souls like), I like this type of game specialy because the sensation of necessity of strategy to win the bosses, the fact of you need to memorize the patters of movement and the powers you'll need to defeat the boss it's awesome.

That said, the last valkiria it's an awesome place to start in souls like, what a difcult boss dude!

This game was amazing in every aspect! EXCEPT FOR THE ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ PUZZLES.  

ANWAY! Great game! Must play if you haven't yet!
Peak game! Amazing story. Great Graphics. Would 100% recommend!
The fighting mechanics in this game can be hot garbage sometimes but overall the story and visuals make up for this fault. a solid 8.8/10

One of the greatest pieces of media ever created has come to PC. Even if you are not a fighting game fan, this game is still enjoyable.

The narrative of God of War should be studied by media scholars. The dialogue is brilliantly written and brought to life by a fantastic group of VA's. Every character has their own personality that sticks with you, which is difficult to do as a writer. They're not stupid for the sake of the plot and their flaws make sense. All of this combines into a beautifully character driven tale. The plot is somewhat simple but you're taken through a gorgeous view of Nordic and Greek mythology along a thrilling emotional roller coaster.

Game play is satisfying and can be challenging. I am not a fighting game person, and I will fully admit to struggling on normal difficulty. Yet, I never brought it down because it made winning the fights so much more satisfying; and as I became better at the game, it just felt so symbiotic with the narrative. The keybindings all make sense, but you can tailor them to your liking. For the most part, it all feels fluid, Kratos does what you expect, and the combat is satisfying. There will be times of frustration, especially with certain bosses, but that's typical of the genre and something you can overcome. 

On a technical level, the game only had 1 hard crash on me, but was otherwise smooth and satisfying. Key binds have options, the graphics menus have a fair selection of options, and the quality of the port is overall good. There are no standout missing features that would make this a poor PC experience.

Value wise, this is a good deal, even at full price. I understand some hesitancy from consumers to buy a single player, semi-linear, narrative game as the replay value may be lower. However, the quality of the experience so great that you will not regret this purchase whatsoever. Additionally, the additional side quests are generally satisfying that add value. With new game plus as an option, I can easily see 2 or even 3 plays being appealing as you may want to try a harder difficulty as you improve.
overall amazing open world game, would be better if there's bosses or valk, you could spawn to try your builds. once you finish the story mode there's pretty small things you can do.

Gow 2018 is one of the most emotional/impactful stories I have ever consumed. The sheer emotion in every line, the voice/motion capture, the incredible themes that transcends culture and language. I consider this game an incredible work of art and it will always have a special place in my heart, only seconded to its own sequel GOW of Ragnarok which simply improves on every single and I do mean every single thing from the 2018 game. 

The content is not too repetitive, its engaging, unique and the dialogue, voice lines, stories told on the boat and exposure to context and lore, not to mention the stunning visuals are more advantages for this game. 

A must play. This is how you revive a franchise.

Peak game. 

This is one of the best story games I have played. The combat is fun and the story is amazing, but it is not without some imperfections. Some parts of the story are boring but most of it is awesome. This game starts slow but picks up fast so don't judge it right away. My biggest critique is every non boss enemy feels the same, there is probably in total 15-20  different bases for grunt enemies and mini bosses that are just reskinned throughout the game.

Combat-fun
Story-absolute cinema
exploration-rewarding
side quests/optional content- great

Yeah, I recommend it. Mixed feelings overall, but leaning towards positive.

Visually, the game is completely wonderful. There are a bunch of details in almost every corner, with great presentation whenever you encounter something new. Major boss fights are honestly amazing and are by far the biggest highlight of the entire thing.

Combat is solid but can feel rather boring at the start due to the lack of skills unlocked. You need to level up your weapon first and then use XP, which is fine, but by the end of the game I had the vast majority of skills unlocked with an extra 50K sitting there, and that's considering I still had a lot left to explore. The normal bosses were also pretty cool at first, but then you realize they are mainly the same Ancients and Trolls with a different coat of paint, which eventually took me from excited to just rolling my eyes every time I saw one.

The map is massive and there are plenty of side quests to do but to be fair, most of these boil down to: kill some bad guys, grab an item and come back. A lot of areas are locked behind certain story progression, and before you unlock fast travel (waaaay too late in the campaign), you will have a not so fun time going back and forth. Seeing a Valkyrie (superboss) or a Dragon for the first time is awesome but I ain't the type of guy to enjoy doing the same mission with a couple of slight changes.

You can also really tell this game is a console Sony exclusive. Mainly due to it trying to be as cinematic as possible, and because it has dull moments of camera lock slow-walk animation sections to load the next area without you noticing. Felt like a walking simulator at times (which is expected from Sony). I also don't really think they needed to make this game an RPG with a bunch of chests everywhere. I do appreciate the puzzles, some of them are quite clever and original, but a lot of them feel repetitive. You have an interesting moment happen just to be looting a chest while the characters are still talking about it lol. This, combined with the side quests, hammered the point in my mind that the game is trying to pad game time.

Atreus' (the boy) main job seems to be to hype me up, give obvious hints and forcefully contrast with Kratos' personality at any given chance. No child speaks like this, and at times it can feel genuinely annoying. Does that mean I hate him? Nah, I enjoyed the story quite a lot, but it felt like they heavily nerfed Kratos so he wouldn't just Gangnam Style on everyone as soon as they showed up. It also had no logic sometimes: [spoiler] Kratos attempting to hide the fact that he is a god while he is moving entire buildings, or how when he finally reveals it to the boy, Atreus literally doesn't care at all [/spoiler].

Anyway, I bought this on sale for 20 EUR. At that price it's totally worth it. Full price, maybe wait for a sale. I just wish it had less padding, fewer repeated bosses, and less RPG clutter pretending to add depth.

This is one of those games that makes it hard to play other games. I don't understand the negative reviews, this game has a good variety of combat and great story. The movements and fighting mechanics are well done, I'm impressed by how it feels to fight, its really fun and makes your character feel strong but not overpowered to where it gets boring. This  game is truly one of my favorite games of all time

God of War is just Kratos saying “Boy” in 37 different emotional tones while brutally destroying Norse mythology.

Came for the axe combat, stayed for the accidental therapy session.
10/10 — now I throw things and expect them to come back.
very good game, every time I played it I got hooked and played for as long as possible. Nice storyline and plenty of side content to do after finishing the story
game is very fun in all aspects such as gameplay with a wide variety in the combat, graphic are good and story is moving overall banger but not better than rdr2
I've done like 3 playthroughs. Its a good game with lots of content and a replay is always fun every few years. (I used to play on my ps4 before i got my pc)

Great game. 9/10.

Only nitpicks is the lack of variety of enemies, and the weird progression where you keep going back and forth on the same path with some detours.
Very pleased I purchased this. Really Immersive and the right amount of difficulty to keep things interesting and on your toes. Such an excellent game in soo many ways.
If you like the series, it's worth it. It's very refreshing to have some new mechanics along with the story.
To start off, this game is beautiful, graphics look great considering this game came out 8 years ago. I think you will like the game :))
A brutal guy with an axe and a child is wandering through realms, solving puzzles and smashing chests — and desperately in need of some parenting advice along the way.

English :

Kratos has rebuilt his life in Odin’s Nordic lands and tries not to repeat the same mistakes as before, accompanied by his son Atreus. The story of this God of War is much more intimate than the first trilogy’s entries, somewhat relegating the Nordic pantheon to the background and using it to serve the game’s central theme: the father-son relationship between the two protagonists. It’s very well done, and the narrative manages to engage the player deeply.

This game lets us explore, alongside Kratos and his son, several realms from Norse mythology in a very successful semi-open world, both visually and in terms of level design.

Gameplay-wise, it’s simple but very effective, becoming more complex as the adventure progresses. It’s a real pleasure to smash faces with a Kratos who remains as visceral as ever.

However, there’s a noticeable lack of variety in the bestiary, and the pacing is less effective than in previous entries. The only truly memorable fights are the first and the last battles of the story.

The soundtrack is understated but rather successful, though no piece really left a lasting impression on me.

God of War (2018) is an experience worth having. The game successfully captures everything that made the franchise strong originally while reinventing it, both in gameplay and storytelling.

_____

Français :

Kratos a refait sa vie dans les contrées nordiques d'Odin et essaie de ne pas répéter les mêmes erreurs qu'auparavant, accompagné de son fils Atreus. L'histoire de ce God Of War se veut beaucoup plus intimiste que les opus de la première trilogie, reléguant un peu au second plan le panthéon nordique et l'utilisant pour servir le propos du jeu : la relation père-fils des deux protagonistes. C'est très réussi et la narration parvient à impliquer le joueur dans celle-ci.

Ce jeu nous permet de découvrir, aux côtés de Kratos et de son fils, plusieurs royaumes issus de la mythologie nordique, dans un monde semi-ouvert très réussi, que ce soit visuellement ou au niveau du level design.

Côté gameplay, on a quelque chose de simple mais très efficace, qui se complexifie au fil de l'aventure. C'est un véritable plaisir de détruire des gueules avec un Kratos toujours aussi viscéral.

On regrettera cependant un manque flagrant de diversité dans le bestiaire, ainsi qu'un rythme moins efficace que dans les opus précédents. On ne retient de mémorable que le premier et le dernier combat du scénario.

La bande-son est discrète mais plutôt réussie, bien qu'aucune musique ne m'ait véritablement marqué.

God of War (2018) est une expérience a faire. Le jeu a réussi à capturer tout ce qui faisait la force de la licence à l’origine, tout en la réinventant, que ce soit au niveau du gameplay ou de la narration.
Not the best GoW, but still a great game with a great narrative.
genuinely such a great game. It's beautiful, it's fun, it's educational (sorta!), and most of all, it's inspiring.
I like it, I haven't even finished it yet, but I can say it is definetely going to be one of my all time favourites. 9/10

Fun, engaging epic feeling combat and a little bit of a good father son adventure story. Plenty of extras to do or not and variable level of difficulty you can set at any time. Not too long and looking forward to playing the next one.

This game was great, the graphics were amazing, the story was just was perfect down to the last minute detail.
If you can get it, get it.

Genuine masterpiece of a game. One of very few 10/10s in my opinion. I played in high school on my ps4, then swapped to a steam purchase, so I truly have no clue how many hours I have in this game. The soundtrack, the story, the gameplay, the art direction - it's all so, so perfect.
this is the best game in the whole wide world. The storyline is AMAZING to die for! I would even give my unborn child just to have  2nd edition. DONT TELL MY GIRLFRIEND

Incredible game, beat it years ago and decided to comeback and attempt a 100% file. Just finished the main story again, and i just am in awe all over again. One of those games where im sad that its almost over. Obv story, animation, voice acting, all 10/10. The combat is very fun but a bit button mashy in my opinion, still pretty great tho.   I cant wait to play the second game. I made the mistake of googleing reviews and fell into a reddit rabit hole of people saying that its a terrible game because its basically just a super boring movie, and i couldnt dissagree more. Honestly, the long cutscenes are probably my favorite part. They jsut do so much to show the charectors interacting, and show us more about the world. Overall a must play, probably like a 9.5 out of 10.

7/10 - Decent game, but doesn't hold up to the scale of God of War 1, 2 and of course the GOAT...3, feels way more like they took ideas from the other 3rd person Sony franchises and drags on instead of being huge in scale late game....Combat really suffers from not being like the OGs as the camera design makes it feel clunky.

An entreteining game rich in lore and gameplay
While I find the game entretaining the game is bad in terms of being just or unjust. I played it in "Give me God of War" as the first try and found are parts of the gameplay not related to the dificulty that are unjust, for example, in a part of Alfhaim you fight a lot of Dark Elfs who can directly combo you into a 2 hit death

Great story, Amazing cinematics and the game looks gorgeous, but the fighting it self can get a boring quiet easily, and you find your self playing the game just for the story and runing through the fights. The puzzles can be fun, but they get repetitive as well because they are pretty much the same.

I played through God of War (2018) when it first released on PS4, and it's still one of the most affecting games I've ever experienced. The story of Kratos and Atreus is deceptively simple on the surface, just a father and son carrying ashes up a mountain, but underneath it's a quietly devastating exploration of loss, grief, and trying to be better than the man you used to be. The Norse setting is stunning, the Leviathan Axe might be the single most satisfying weapon in any game, and the reinvention of the franchise pulled off something almost no series ever manages. Like with Spider-Man, the sequel just couldn't quite live up to what came before, but that's not really the point. This game is about the journey, and every award and accolade it picked up was completely deserved. If you've got the hours and you somehow haven't played it yet, do yourself a favor and dive in. It will not disappoint.

Great game. I beat it on PS4 first, but years later on PC, with a mouse and keyboard, 120FPS, 4K, it's almost a completely new experience. 

Some of the enemies are just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ annoying, though, like the revenants and the tatzelwurms. 

I'd also like more transparency in what the stats do; does 1 strength increase melee damage by 1 damage, 1%, or what? For example, there is a hidden stagger mechanic in addition to the visible stun meter; some attacks can stagger some enemies and Kratos, but the same attack might not always stagger the same enemy; this moving of the goalposts is a sin in any game, if you ask me. 

Otherwise, the story, graphics, and general moment-to-moment gameplay is awesome and very more-ish. I look forward to playing Ragnarok.

The original God of War trilogy had a special place in my heart as a fast-paced hack and slash game with grandiose boss battles and fun Olympian lore. If you come in with the expectations for this to play out similarly, you will sadly be disappointed. The GOW reboot foregoes this iconic formula in favor of a more… “methodical” gameplay infused with RPG elements.

Maybe comparison is the thief of joy, but when a game is a reboot, it’s difficult not to pit the new against the old.

Firstly, I’ll start with what worked: the story. They did a phenomenal job in intertwining lore and breathing life into the world. Elements such as the plot itself and character development are well established. There are some moments that really shine where you’ve seen how far the characters have come and makes some of the more… sour moments feel more acceptable.

Honestly, as much as I wanted to love this game, the story is where my personal praise stops. I’ll do my best to treat it as objectively and stand-alone as possible. My main criticisms are due to the friction between one simple fact: how I want to play vs. how the game forces you to play it. I LOATHED my first 10 hours of gameplay and then the rest became tolerable with some shining moments sprinkled throughout.

1) Gameplay/power fantasy feels incredibly underwhelming. And I think it’s a mix of different things – Kratos, the god of ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ war, starts off incredibly weak, which I think really hits when you watch a cutscene of Kratos splitting the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Earth against another god, but struggling to kill mobs. I don’t think they highlight enough how IMPORTANT item level is to fighting – it’s the difference between getting two shotted and taking 25 leviathan axe whacks to kill one single enemy compared to obliterating them when you’re 2 levels higher fighting the same opponent. Furthermore, some of the enemy mechanics are literally just plain annoying. They avoid you until you hit them with an arrow. Then you get 3 hits in, and they do the same thing, repeatedly. And some you hit further away than Kratos can catch up to. Some enemies (esp. Valkyries) feel like some of their moves are just literally unavoidable. Some require perfect timing on a dodge; some you have 3 seconds to react to. It’s very unbalanced IMO and just feels… unfair? This is coming from someone who adores the souls genre and challenging gameplay.

2) Loading animations and obstacle traversal being more annoying than needed. Good god, the amount of loading animations is ridiculous. Some zones you are stuck moving from one room to another opening a door, lifting a pillar, every 10-15 seconds. Then some zones are WIDE open. Even this feels unbalanced/not well optimized. Fast traveling should not be from point 1 to 1.5 to 2. Put up a loading screen. Mimir doesn’t need to interrupt me every time I try to get somewhere quickly, please make it stop. As far as obstacle traversal, you’d figure a god that survives hundred foot drops and launches objects into space can jump 10 feet. But no, instead you’re met with a metric ♥♥♥♥ ton of climbing sequences that make a potential 2-3 second, 10x as long. And this accumulates when backtracking to find collectibles. I get why they do it, it extends game time and they need to compensate for Atreus, but I do feel like they could’ve approached it more elegantly without making it feel like 2/51 hours of gameplay time was purely traversing walls.

3) Although less problematic, this had to be pointed out – during a live cutscene, you're slowed down considerably and your camera locks to focus on one thing. Good god, this could not be more irritating. 3 seconds of this sends enough of a message. Please, allow people to freely move their cameras. This only adds to the friction I was already experiencing and did nothing for me but make me feel like I was locked onto a railway being fed useless information.

4) Another point of pain was SOME of the story, even if it was the only redeeming thing. [b]Spoilers here[/b]: Freya mystically turning because of the whole Baldur interaction made no sense and was justified as “only a parent knows”. Like bruh. Let’s be so for real. I also understand Atreus is meant to grow throughout the story, but his bratty phase really had me thinking “please stfu” after his 30th smart remark. Then when he finds out about his true nature, he devolves into an egomaniac, and one fight sets him straight. For me, this was a bit annoying but far more forgivable than the general gameplay.

I’ve already made this too long, but [b]TLDR[/b]: gameplay is unintuitive, slow, and unbalanced. It feels like devs do not respect your time by modifying certain elements to force you to play the game longer than needed. It’s not an overall catastrophe and the story is well written with good character progression, but I personally would never recommend it unless it’s on sale for $10-20.

With over 200 hours in the Playstation version I can safely say God of War is one of the greatest games of all time.  With a level of story telling that only Sony Santa Monica can deliver, this reinvention of Kratos is an epic journey that's hard to put down.  Absolute must play!
wet dreams about christopher judge every night since playing this. would recommend.

I understand that the graphics can be a positive thing, BUT if you want good graphics with fantastic gameplay and story, just play Red Dead Redemption 2 instead!
